channel.title: Anthropic on Learnings in IT
channel.description: Recent content in Anthropic on Learnings in IT

item.title: Agentic Analytics for Data warehouse using local models
item.description:
Introduction

Traditionally, Business Intelligence (BI) involves custom built dashboards, reports that query datawarehouse or BI Tools to do the same. This involves involvement/intervention by engineering team to analyze requirements and spend time to develop, deliver and maintain required artifacts (i.e. dashboards, Reports etc.). Lets say your data ware house is built on SQL based DBMS and you have custom-built Web interface for Analytics and reporting.

What are the typical challenges? - Time consuming change management - Engineering team intervention needed for ad-hoc requirements for reports/dashboard or any specific analytics. - Lack of flexibility - Need for faster go to market (GTM)

item.title: Notes on Excellent AI Agent Architecture via A2A/MCP by Jeffrey Richter
item.description:
Introduction

This article compiles my notes on an excellent architectural deep-dive by Jeffrey Richter. He outlines how to architect robust AI Agents using Google’s Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol for coordination and Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool integration.

It is a must-read for anyone moving beyond simple chatbots to complex agentic workflows. You can read the original article here.
